summary: The repo has two critical bugs related to authentication and one stale documentation issue. Security should be the top priority this week.

clusters.summary: Two issues related to OAuth token handling and session expiry.
Outdated API reference and missing quickstart guide.

top_priorities.title: OAuth tokens expire silently
top_priorities.reasoning: Users lose sessions without warning. 15 reactions and a maintainer confirmation in comments indicate widespread impact.
top_priorities.suggested_action: Assign to auth team lead and schedule a hotfix for the next patch release.

top_priorities.title: Session cookie not rotated after login
top_priorities.reasoning: Session fixation vulnerability. Lower confidence because reproduction steps are incomplete.
top_priorities.suggested_action: Ask reporter for a minimal reproduction case, then triage with the security team.

top_priorities.title: CLI crashes on empty config file
top_priorities.reasoning: Crash on a common edge case. 3 comments confirming the issue.
top_priorities.suggested_action: Add a guard clause in config loader — good candidate for a quick fix.

stale_issues.title: Update API reference for v2
stale_issues.reason: No activity in 180 days. The v2 API has since shipped — this issue may be obsolete.

quick_wins.title: Add quickstart guide to README
quick_wins.why_quick: Well-scoped documentation task with a clear acceptance criterion. No code changes required.

duplicate_groups.reasoning: Both describe authentication token lifecycle bugs — #103 is a specific sub-case of #101.
